codeReview.summary: The diff correctly preserves the Welcome CTA behavior, adds the explicit `Get started` accessibility anchor, and marks the Welcome title as a header; current HEAD's auth-screen implementations also still expose the required literal anchors and header roles. I did not find a blocking functional or accessibility regression in the reviewed feature, but the updated Welcome copy still uses iOS-specific 'secure enclave' wording on a cross-platform screen.

issues.description: The new feature-row copy says the wallet is 'stored in the secure enclave', but this screen ships on both iOS and Android. Mission architecture documents iOS storage as Keychain and Android storage as Keystore (`.factory/library/architecture.md:152-166`), so the shared copy is now materially platform-specific/inaccurate for Android users. Consider changing this sentence to platform-neutral wording such as secure hardware / secure storage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mobile-feature-worker` skill's honesty gate is still not being enforced consistently for anchor-focused UI work: this worker reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true` even though the transcript skeleton does not show the required library reads for a test-running anchor feature, and the edits landed before any new failing test was demonstrated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:20-29` requires reading `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md` when running tests, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` for user-facing anchor assertions, plus writing failing tests first. The transcript skeleton at `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/worker-transcripts.jsonl:37` shows reads of `AGENTS.md`, `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, screen files, and test runs, then `Edit` calls for `src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.tsx` before the later `Create` of `src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.test.tsx`; that skeleton does not show reads of `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, or `.factory/library/user-testing.md`. The handoff still records `followedProcedure: true` at `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/handoffs/2026-04-22T22-00-24-071Z__welcome-and-accessibility-anchors__32a48393-804a-47e6-bec8-6427a0f41d0b.json:89-91`.

summary: Pass: the reviewed diff satisfies the Welcome-screen accessibility-anchor work and does not introduce a blocking regression, while the existing auth screens still cover the other required anchors. Non-blocking follow-up: make the updated Welcome security copy platform-neutral instead of referring to a 'secure enclave' on the shared iOS/Android screen.
